title: GENDER RULES (el vs la)
content_json: {"column_headers": ["Ending", "Gender", "Example", "Exceptions", "Reliability"], "rows": [["-o", "el (masculine)", "el libro, el gato, el banco", "la mano, la foto, la moto", "Very High"], ["-a", "la (feminine)", "la casa, la mesa, la playa", "el día, el mapa, el sofá", "Very High"], ["-ción / -sión", "la (always feminine)", "la nación, la televisión, la educación", "No exceptions", "100%"], ["-dad / -tad", "la (always feminine)", "la ciudad, la universidad, la libertad", "No exceptions", "100%"], ["-ma (Greek origin)", "el (masculine!)", "el problema, el sistema, el programa, el tema", "Trips up everyone. Looks feminine but isn't", "Very High"], ["-ista", "el/la (matches person)", "el/la artista, el/la turista", "Gender matches the person, not the word", "Rule"], ["-aje", "el (masculine)", "el viaje, el garaje, el mensaje", "Very few exceptions", "Very High"], ["-or", "el (masculine)", "el color, el amor, el doctor", "la flor, la labor", "High"], ["-ión", "la (feminine)", "la opinión, la región, la unión", "el avión, el camión", "High"], ["-umbre", "la (feminine)", "la costumbre, la cumbre, la muchedumbre", "No common exceptions", "Very High"], ["-ez / -eza", "la (feminine)", "la vez, la belleza, la naturaleza", "el pez (fish)", "High"], ["-ente / -ante", "el/la (matches person)", "el/la estudiante, el/la cantante", "Gender matches the person", "Rule"], ["-ismo", "el (masculine)", "el capitalismo, el turismo", "No exceptions", "100%"], ["-miento", "el (masculine)", "el movimiento, el sentimiento", "No exceptions", "100%"]], "sub_headers": null, "footnotes": null}

title: "I HAVE ___" = HE + ado/ido (works just like English)

title: WEIRDO: When to use subjunctive (opposite vowel endings)
content_json: {"column_headers": ["Letter", "Trigger", "Example (indicative → subjunctive)", "Why subjunctive?", "The swap"], "rows": [["W", "Wishes / Wants", "Quiero que vengas (I want you to come)", "You WANT it but it's not confirmed reality", "-AR→E endings, -ER/-IR→A endings"], ["E", "Emotions", "Me alegra que estés aquí (I'm glad you're here)", "You FEEL something about someone's action", "Swap the vowel"], ["I", "Impersonal expressions", "Es importante que estudies (It's important you study)", "'It is' + adjective + que = subjunctive", "Swap the vowel"], ["R", "Recommendations / Requests", "Te recomiendo que pruebes esto (I recommend you try this)", "You're SUGGESTING, not stating a fact", "Swap the vowel"], ["D", "Doubt / Denial", "No creo que sea verdad (I don't think it's true)", "You DOUBT it, so it's not confirmed", "Swap the vowel"], ["O", "Ojalá", "Ojalá que funcione (I hope it works)", "Ojalá ALWAYS triggers subjunctive", "Swap the vowel"]], "sub_headers": null, "footnotes": null}

title: THE VOWEL SWAP RULE: how to form the subjunctive
content_json: {"column_headers": ["Verb type", "Normal ending", "Subjunctive ending", "Example", "In a sentence"], "rows": [["-AR verbs (yo)", "-o", "-e", "hablo → hable", "Quiero que hable = I want him to speak"], ["-AR verbs (tú)", "-as", "-es", "hablas → hables", "Espero que hables = I hope you speak"], ["-AR verbs (él)", "-a", "-e", "habla → hable", "Dudo que hable = I doubt he speaks"], ["-AR verbs (nosotros)", "-amos", "-emos", "hablamos → hablemos", "Es mejor que hablemos = Better we talk"], ["-AR verbs (ellos)", "-an", "-en", "hablan → hablen", "Quiero que hablen = I want them to speak"], ["-ER/-IR verbs (yo)", "-o", "-a", "como → coma", "No quiero que coma eso = I don't want him to eat that"], ["-ER/-IR verbs (tú)", "-es", "-as", "comes → comas", "Espero que comas bien = I hope you eat well"], ["-ER/-IR verbs (él)", "-e", "-a", "come → coma", "Dudo que coma = I doubt he eats"], ["-ER/-IR verbs (nosotros)", "-emos/-imos", "-amos", "comemos → comamos", "Es hora que comamos = Time we eat"], ["-ER/-IR verbs (ellos)", "-en", "-an", "comen → coman", "Quiero que coman = I want them to eat"], ["MEMORY TRICK", "A endings?", "Swap to E", "-AR verbs flip A→E", "Subjunctive = opposite vowel. That's it."], [null, "E endings?", "Swap to A", "-ER/-IR verbs flip E→A", "They switch jerseys."]], "sub_headers": null, "footnotes": null}

title: LONERS / DIJON: gender shortcut (el vs la)
content_json: {"column_headers": ["Mnemonic", "Endings", "Gender", "Examples", "Exceptions to watch"], "rows": [["L", "-l", "el (masculine)", "el papel, el animal, el hotel, el árbol", "la miel (honey), la sal (salt)"], ["O", "-o", "el (masculine)", "el libro, el gato, el banco, el vino", "la mano, la foto, la moto"], ["N", "-n", "el (masculine)", "el pan, el tren, el jardín, el corazón", "la razón, la imagen"], ["E", "-e", "el (masculine)", "el coche, el café, el puente, el nombre", "la calle, la noche, la clase, la leche"], ["R", "-r", "el (masculine)", "el color, el amor, el lugar, el dolor", "la flor, la mujer, la labor"], ["S", "-s", "el (masculine)", "el mes, el país, el autobús, el lunes", "Few exceptions"], ["D", "-d", "la (feminine)", "la ciudad, la verdad, la salud, la pared", "el césped (lawn)"], ["I", "-ión", "la (feminine)", "la nación, la televisión, la opinión", "el avión, el camión"], ["J", "-a (and -ja/-ga)", "la (feminine)", "la casa, la mesa, la vida, la cosa", "el día, el mapa, el problema, el sistema"], ["ON", "-ón (but -ción is feminine!)", "la for -ción", "la canción, la estación, la acción", "el corazón, el ratón (plain -ón = masculine)"], [null, "SUMMARY", "Guys are LONERS", "Words ending in L, O, N, E, R, S → usually el", null], [null, null, "Girls like DIJON", "Words ending in D, -ión, A, -ción → usually la", null]], "sub_headers": null, "footnotes": null}

title: VIN DIESEL: irregular command verbs (tú imperative)
content_json: {"column_headers": ["Mnemonic word", "Command (tú)", "Infinitive", "Meaning", "Example"], "rows": [["Vin", "Ven", "venir (to come)", "Come!", "¡Ven aquí! = Come here!"], ["Diesel", "Di", "decir (to say)", "Say! / Tell!", "¡Dime la verdad! = Tell me the truth!"], ["Has", "Sal", "salir (to leave)", "Get out! / Leave!", "¡Sal de ahí! = Get out of there!"], [null, "Haz", "hacer (to do/make)", "Do! / Make!", "¡Haz tu tarea! = Do your homework!"], ["Ten", "Ten", "tener (to have)", "Have! / Hold!", "¡Ten cuidado! = Be careful! (Have care!)"], ["Weapons", "Ve", "ir (to go)", "Go!", "¡Ve a la tienda! = Go to the store!"], [null, "Pon", "poner (to put)", "Put!", "¡Pon la mesa! = Set the table! (Put the table!)"], ["Eh?", "Sé", "ser (to be)", "Be!", "¡Sé fuerte! = Be strong!"]], "sub_headers": ["\"Vin Diesel Has Ten Weapons, Eh?\" = Ven Di Sal Haz Ten Ve Pon Sé"], "footnotes": ["WHY THIS MATTERS: most tú commands just use the él/ella form (habla, come, escribe). These 8 are the ONLY ones that break that rule."]}
